howItWorks.intro: This fuel cost per mile calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Cost per Mile
rules.explanation: Divide fuel price by miles per gallon.

howItWorks.outro: Vehicle specifications and costs vary by model year and condition. Consult your mechanic for specific advice.

faq.question: What is the average cost per mile?
faq.answer: At $3.50/gal and 28 MPG, that's about 12.5 cents per mile for fuel alone.

faq.question: What about total cost per mile?
faq.answer: Including insurance, maintenance, and depreciation, the IRS rate is 67 cents/mile (2024).

faq.question: Is the Fuel Cost Per Mile Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard automotive care and maintenance formulas trusted by car owners and enthusiasts. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What fuel economy should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate fuel economy value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

contentIntro: Most drivers know their car gets a certain MPG but have no idea what each mile actually costs them in fuel. At $3.50 per gallon and 25 MPG, every mile costs 14 cents in fuel alone. Commuting 30 miles round trip costs $4.20 per day or $1,092 per year — just in gas. This calculator converts your vehicle's fuel economy and local gas prices into a per-mile cost, plus projects daily, monthly, and annual fuel expenses based on your driving patterns. It is essential for comparing vehicles, deciding whether a fuel-efficient car is worth the premium, or calculating the true cost of a long commute.
howToSteps: Enter your vehicle's fuel economy in MPG — check the sticker or fueleconomy.gov for your model
Set your local gas price per gallon
Enter your typical daily or weekly miles driven
View your cost per mile, daily fuel cost, and annual fuel expense
Compare against a more efficient vehicle to see potential savings
tipsAndConsiderations: The difference between 15 MPG and 25 MPG saves more fuel than the difference between 25 MPG and 50 MPG. Replacing a gas guzzler has the biggest impact.
Highway MPG is typically 20-30% better than city MPG. A car rated 28 city/36 highway might average 30 MPG in mixed driving.
Tire pressure, driving speed, and maintenance significantly affect real-world MPG. Under-inflated tires alone can reduce fuel economy by 3-5%.
At current gas prices, the break-even on a hybrid premium of $3,000-$5,000 is typically 4-6 years for average drivers.
tips: Double-check your fuel economy before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both cost per mile and annual fuel cost — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to maintain your vehicle.
If you're unsure about your annual miles driven, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
